text.title: Sonatype Central Portal + GPG Signing
text.summary: Use the post-OSSRH Central Portal endpoint and JReleaser's signing block; never hand-roll `nexus-staging-maven-plugin`.

section.title: Central Portal is mandatory in 2026
section.p: Sonatype OSSRH was retired June 30 2025; new and existing namespaces must publish via the Central Portal (`https://central.sonatype.com/api/v1/publisher`). JReleaser ships a `mavenCentral` deployer for this endpoint; the legacy `nexus2` deployer points at OSSRH and no longer works for new releases.

rule.statement: `jreleaser.yml` MUST configure `deploy.maven.mavenCentral.sonatype` with `url: https://central.sonatype.com/api/v1/publisher` and `applyMavenCentralRules: true`. Signing MUST be enabled (`signing.active: ALWAYS`, `signing.armored: true`, with GPG key + passphrase in env vars / OIDC, never in the file). Agents are forbidden from setting `signing.active: NEVER` to "fix" a CI failure.
rule.rationale: OSSRH-targeted configs deploy nowhere in 2026; Central Portal rules (POM completeness, sources/javadoc jars, signed checksums) are stricter than legacy OSSRH and JReleaser bakes them in.

example.code:
signing: active: ALWAYS armored: true mode: MEMORY # key from env, not filesystem
deploy: maven: mavenCentral: sonatype: active: ALWAYS url: https://central.sonatype.com/api/v1/publisher applyMavenCentralRules: true stagingRepositories: - target/staging-deploy
release: github: overwrite: true draft: false changelog: formatted: ALWAYS preset: conventional-commits contributors: format: "- {{contributorName}} ({{contributorUsernameAsLink}})"
example.note: Central Portal endpoint, signed deploy, conventional-commit notes generated automatically.

example.code: deploy: maven: nexus2: maven-central: active: ALWAYS url: https://oss.sonatype.org/service/local # retired snapshotUrl: https://oss.sonatype.org/content/repositories/snapshots signing: active: NEVER # disabled to make CI green
example.note: Endpoint is dead; signing-disabled release will be rejected by Central Portal anyway.

antipattern.description: Storing the GPG passphrase in `jreleaser.yml` so the agent can run releases locally for testing.
antipattern.reason: Secrets in source control leak forever; use `JRELEASER_GPG_PASSPHRASE` env var, repository secret, or OIDC, and run dry-runs against `target/staging-deploy` instead.
